story: Matthieu meets Nora in a café and tells himself not to idealise her, but he does. Later, after a night together, she says he doesn’t know her and sets a hard limit: Your intensity is inappropriate. He keeps texting, half-apologising, insisting on his story. Nora locks the chain, closes the curtains, leaves the stove light on. She messages her downstairs neighbor, Karim, who works nights as a security guard; he replies he’s in the lobby with another resident and has already called the police—patrol is a few minutes out. Matthieu pulls at her building’s door—locked—then slips in behind a stranger, wedges his shoe to hold it. He sits on the stair, rehearses, then stands. He walks to her floor and raises his hand to the door.
implausible_knowledge: She messages her downstairs neighbor, Karim, who works nights as a security guard; he replies he’s in the lobby with another resident and has already called the police—patrol is a few minutes out.
question: When Matthieu reaches Nora’s door on the landing, what is he most likely to do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Leave the building immediately without knocking to avoid the arriving police.
answer_without_knowledge: Knock on Nora’s door and try to talk to her.
